response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled software craftsman with 15+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, TypeScript/Node.js, and high-performance backend systems — not C++. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, with no production C++ work in the past decade. The role's core requirements — deep C++/STL expertise, industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development practices, and industrial protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT) — are largely absent from his profile, making him a poor fit despite his overall engineering quality.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance concurrent systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and low-latency architectures
Strong software craftsmanship culture including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring through workshops and dojos
Event-driven and distributed systems background (GISCollective, Sociomantic microservices) partially overlaps with message-broker and scalable platform responsibilities
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — only a 6-month NI role in 2014–2015 and a student thesis project; no meaningful C++ production work in over 10 years
No industrial automation, OT/ICS, or IoT domain experience; no exposure to industrial protocols such as Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, or MQTT
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices, IEC 62443 standards, or any security-focused engineering, which is a non-negotiable core requirement for this role

key_factors.factor: No industrial/OT domain or protocol experience
Strong distributed systems and craftsmanship background
